_comment: Data-driven intent classification rules for EN/ICAO-VFR phraseology. Loaded by src/atc/intent_rules.cpp. Schema: see intent_rules.hpp. Facility keywords (tower/ground/information/radio/delivery) are supplied here as has_facility values -- the matcher does a standalone-word match, no hardcoded C++ list. Runway/position tokens (left/right/center/one..thirty six/niner) live in the shared runway_token_suffixes() C++ vector and already cover EN. Note: intent_parser::parse() runs en_phraseology::parse_spoken_number() before this layer, so ICAO digit-by-digit pronunciation ('one zero one tree') already arrives as raw digits ('1013') -- rules below match on numeric form.

normalize._note: Reserved for known Whisper/Voxtral mishearings (EN VFR), analogous to the DE table (GitHub issues #9, #30). INVARIANT: only map non-words that are collision-free under a global substring replace (never a legitimate English word/token). Empty for now; EN STT mishearing tuning is a follow-up.

rules._note: Profanity / insults toward ATC. Highest priority so tone is caught even when other intent keywords are present.
Pilot-issued correction phraseology (ICAO CORRECTION / self-correction). 'negative' alone is ICAO's 'no'; 'negative contact' is a traffic-advisory reply and must NOT be claimed here.
RMZ entry/transit (European radio mandatory zone). 'rmz' anchor keeps it collision-free with INITIAL_CALL_INBOUND.
RMZ leaving. No 'frequency'/'good day' -> collision-free with LEAVING_FREQUENCY.
Pilot-initiated frequency change announcement (leaving frequency has no fixed ICAO phrase; convention).
Traffic-advisory replies must come BEFORE SELF_ANNOUNCE.

Readback. EN phrases: 'wilco'/'roger', ATC-instruction echoes ('contact ground/tower', 'taxi to holding point', 'line up', 'hold short'), and the compact Tower echoes 'cleared to land'/'cleared for take-off' -- STT renders the Tower's wording verbatim, so the pilot's repeat-back shows up as the same tokens. The position/departure echo patterns require the imperative 'report' (the Tower instruction being read back) -- NOT the bare position word: a pilot's own report 'final'/'downwind'/'ready for departure' is a REPORT_POSITION_*/READY_FOR_DEPARTURE act, not a readback, and must fall through to those rules.
VFR cross-country departure: ready-for-departure plus a course/direction marker.

AFIS departure self-announce. Frequency gating (INFO/RADIO) via flight_rules.json intent_frequency. Sits after READY_FOR_DEPARTURE so 'ready for departure' (towered) wins there; the none-guard keeps taxi/landing phrases out.
Parking/apron/ramp as the destination -- NOT pilot's location.

Runway-vacated. ICAO 'runway vacated'. Also accept 'runway'+'vacated' as separate tokens. 'cleared' guard keeps the Tower-echo readbacks ('cleared to land') out -- those are handled by READBACK above.
Plain 'request taxi' / 'taxi'. Exclude clearance readbacks ('taxi to holding point', 'taxi ... via X') and the apron/parking taxi requests handled above.

Position-Reports. Exclude the imperative 'report' (the Tower instruction 'report downwind/base/final') so a pilot readback of that instruction is not misclassified as a position report -- the echo belongs to READBACK.

Inbound/landing request, but only when no facility keyword (tower/information/radio) is present. A facility-addressed call with landing intent is the inbound INITIAL_CALL (INITIAL_CALL_INBOUND below) -- excluding all facility keywords keeps that path reachable at AFIS/Info+Radio fields too, not just towered ones.

Tower/Info/Radio call with inbound/landing intent -- beats INITIAL_CALL_TOWER below. ICAO Doc 4444 12.3.4.13(a)/(d): '[type] (position) (level) FOR LANDING' applies to pattern and cross-country inbound alike. At AFIS fields the pilot addresses 'Information'/'Radio' instead of 'Tower', so all three facility keywords qualify. The mandatory landing-phrase guard keeps this collision-free with the bare INITIAL_CALL_GROUND 'information' rule below (which has no landing phrase) and with the 'information <NATO>' ATIS letter.

AFIS/Info field initial call: pilot addresses '<airport> Information' (no ground/tower). MUST stay after the Tower rules: 'information' also appears as the ATIS letter 'information <NATO>' in towered calls, so an earlier match here would hijack INITIAL_CALL_TOWER. has_facility is a standalone-word match; the on_ground adjustment demotes airborne misfires.
Last-resort fallback for traffic-advisory replies. Single keyword 'looking' is ambiguous; confidence is low.

adjustments._note: Upgrade INITIAL_CALL_INBOUND -> INITIAL_CALL_INBOUND_VRP when a VRP was named.
Airborne intent while on ground -- likely a readback. Demote to READBACK.
Ground intent while airborne -- physically impossible.
SELF_ANNOUNCE only makes sense on UNICOM/CTAF at non-towered fields.
Pilot called 'tower' at a non-towered airport -- phraseology error.
Post-landing 'request taxi' / 'taxi' is a taxi-to-apron request while the pilot is still in the post-landing state window (on the ground, last landing in history, no new DEPARTURE_CLEARED yet).

Post-landing taxi-clearance read-back. The Tower's 'taxi to the GA apron via Alpha' is echoed by the pilot as 'taxi GA apron via Alpha'. That phrasing can miss both READBACK and REQUEST_TAXI_PARKING and land as UNKNOWN. While in the post-landing window, an UNKNOWN utterance that names the apron/parking/ramp destination is the clearance read-back -- accept it silently as READBACK. One entry per destination token because text_contains is a single substring.

RUNWAY_VACATED is physically impossible without a prior airborne phase. With a readback pending, the malformed vacated call was almost certainly the readback the pilot meant. Remap so the tower silently accepts it instead of looping the readback-reminder.

Same physical impossibility (RUNWAY_VACATED without ever being airborne), but no readback pending -- demote confidence to 0.0 so the engine routes the utterance through the LM classify-with-repair path instead of forcing the vacated rule.
